mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the preceding 3 months signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously without requiring a specific trigger threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.3% on 2017-12-01 reflects accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger equity risk-off behavior, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% on 2017-12-01 reflects tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth combined with accelerating retail sales signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both demand components to strengthen simultaneously to activate.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized AND RSAFS growth > 3% YoY
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17163.894B (2017-07-01) and RSAFS at 495381M (2017-12-01) indicate strong demand backdrop
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained demand growth
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) when both GDPC1 and RSAFS growth conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates below 1.5% YoY, signaling supply-side constraints and potential stagflationary pressures that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.3969 (2017-11-01) reflects moderate industrial activity
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite weak industrial production signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates to below 100k new jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains stable, signaling labor market softening that precedes broader economic weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 100k AND UNRATE unchanged or rising
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 147380k (2017-12-01) and UNRATE at 4.1% indicate tight but potentially slowing labor market
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower (t-test p > 0.05) when both payroll deceleration and stable unemployment conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0% and inflation (CPI) accelerates above 2.5% YoY, the Fed enters a tightening cycle that raises real discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.3% and CPIAUCSL at 247.963 (2017-12-01) show early tightening cycle
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite tightening signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline by more than 10% YoY, signaling weakening residential demand that portends broader economic slowdown and reduced corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as forward-looking investors reprice growth.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1297k (2017-11-01) reflects solid housing demand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite housing decline signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates above 3% YoY while payroll employment growth remains above 150k per month, signaling robust supply-side expansion and labor productivity gains that support sustained earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY growth > 3% AND PAYEMS monthly growth > 150k
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.3969 and PAYEMS at 147380k (2017-11-01/12-01) show solid supply-side momentum
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) when both industrial production and payroll growth conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
